role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for coordinating the home health referral and ensuring the family receives clinical training on catheter care, while the Navigator focuses on auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Healing Partners program, and alerting the Social Worker to caregiver anxiety regarding the transition.
narrative_summary: Martha Vance, a 68-year-old woman recovering from a significant multiple sclerosis flare, faced a major transition hurdle when her husband expressed intense anxiety about his lack of training for her new mobility needs. While Martha met her clinical milestones for discharge, her husband's fear of managing her care at home threatened to stall the process. The Navigator acted as a crucial liaison by documenting this caregiver training gap in Atlantis and alerting the Social Worker to the family's need for a bedside demonstration. This ensured that the clinical team addressed the husband's concerns, allowing for a confident move back home with the support of the Healing Partners program.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator audited the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record and identified an error in the primary phone number; the corrected contact information for Martha's husband was entered into Atlantis to ensure seamless communication post-discharge.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with Home Health Agency support.
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator provided the program flyer to Martha's husband and successfully saved the Healing Partners V-Card into his smartphone contacts, explaining that this would help him recognize the caller ID for the first-day follow-up call.
pre_dc_pulse_call: During the 24-hour pre-discharge call, Martha's husband confirmed that the Social Worker had completed the bedside training and they felt ready for the transition, though he requested a reminder of the Medical Assistant's visit window.
atlantis_final_sync: The Navigator recorded the confirmed discharge date in Atlantis and scheduled the Medical Assistant's first home visit for the morning following Martha's return home.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met with Martha's husband, who shared that he had not yet been shown how to assist with her transfers; the Navigator documented this training gap in Atlantis and informed him that the Social Worker would be the best person to coordinate that teaching.

After noticing no updates in the transition plan, the Navigator asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update; the Social Worker noted that the home health agency had been selected but the start-of-care date was pending the completion of family training.

The Social Worker confirmed that the family training was completed and the home health agency accepted the referral for a Monday discharge; the Navigator updated the timeline in Atlantis and began preparing the family for the transition.

format_2_triples.situation: The husband expressed deep concern about his ability to manage Martha's clinical needs.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator documented the caregiver's sentiment in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the family's request for bedside training.

format_2_triples.situation: The family was unclear about how the Healing Partners program would support them once they left the facility.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator explained the role of the Medical Assistant and the importance of the 24-hour post-discharge visit window.

format_2_triples.situation: The Navigator required a status update on the home health agency acceptance to coordinate the next steps.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator asked the Social Worker directly for a verbal update on the referral status and documented the response in Atlantis.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notes that the Social Worker has not yet provided an update on the home health agency's acceptance of the case and chooses to document this communication gap in Atlantis while waiting for a response.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the referral process and avoids unauthorized contact with outside agencies.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the home health referral and simultaneously confirms with the family that they have saved the Healing Partners contact information in their phones.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action stays within the Navigator's lane by gathering information from the appropriate internal source and focusing on family education and program recognition.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency intake department to ask if they have a nurse available who can also provide a quick tutorial on multiple sclerosis flare management for the husband.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because it involves direct contact with a vendor and attempts to influence clinical training, which is the sole responsibility of the facility and the home health agency.
